content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 30,
1992
Mr.
Robert E.
Leidigh
Olson,
Connelly, Hagel,
Fong
& Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-332
Dear Mr.
Leidigh:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(“Act) l/
This advice is sought on behalf of
Lance H.
Olson,
a treasurer for numerous committees.
Because your
letter addresses a general campaign reporting issue,
we consider
it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c)
2/
QUESTION
If a candidate or committee chooses to use a guaranteed
overnight mail delivery service to file a late contribution report
or late independent expenditure report,
when are the reports
required to be mailed and is the delivery service limited to the
United States Post Office?
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3).)
428 j Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
a Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322-566C


Leidigh, Robert
June 30,
1992
Page
2
CONCLUSION
Within 24 hours of making or receiving a late contribution or
making a late independent expenditure,
a candidate or committee
must deposit the appropriate late report with any commercial
guaranteed overnight delivery service.
ANALYSIS
Section 84203(b)
states:
(b)
A late contribution shall be reported by
mailgram, telegram, guaranteed overnight mail through
the United States Postal Service,
or personal delivery
within 24 hours of the time it is made ir the case of
the candidate or committee that makes the contribution
and within 24 hours of the time it is received in the
case of the recipient...
Section 84204(a)
states:
(a)
A candidate or committee that makes a late
independent expenditure,
as defined in Section 82036.5,
shall report the late independent expenditure by
mailgram,
telegram, guaranteed overnight mail through
the United States Postal Service or personal delivery
within 24 hours of the time it is made...
A candidate or committee that chooses to use a guaranteed
overnight mail delivery system for reporting late contributions or
late independent expenditures must, within 24 hours of making or
receiving a late contribution or making a late independent
expenditure, deposit the appropriate report with a delivery
service.
The statute does not require that such reports arrive at
the filing officer’s location within 24 hours of making or
receiving a late contribution or making a late independent
expenditure.
Because the Commission has determined that the U.S. Postal
Service does not provide guaranteed overnight delivery service to
some cities, thus depriving voters of timely public information,
any guaranteed overnight delivery service may be used.
(Bagatelos
Advice Letter,
No. A-86-305.
3
/)
Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available
in many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials
and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission
at a cost of l0 per page.


Leidigh, Robert
June 30,
1992
Page
3
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916)
322—5662 if you
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

guaranteed overnight delivery service.
